message:
perf(pager): clean WAL-mode publication-refresh fast path for prepared reads

Adds a specialized `refresh_published_snapshot_for_clean_wal_read`
helper on `SimplePager` and threads it through `PagerBackend` and
`Connection::refresh_memdb_if_stale_with_publication_and_mode`'s
`clean_file_backed_memdb_staleness_check` scenario.

Rationale:

The general `refresh_published_snapshot` path takes the database-file
shared lock and probes the rollback journal as part of every refresh.
This is required when the pager might be in rollback-journal mode or
when rollback recovery is pending — under those conditions the WAL
state alone is not enough to learn the cross-process visibility
horizon.

The prepared-read clean-file-backed-memdb fast path only fires after
the connection has already verified that the pager is in WAL mode
with clean rollback-journal state, the prepared statement is one of
the simple memdb-safe shapes (SimpleRowidLookup, SimpleProjectedRowid
Lookup, SimpleIndexedEqualityLookup, SimpleRowidRangeScan,
SimpleCountStarRowidRange — see `PreparedQueryFastPath::can_use_clean
_file_backed_memdb`), and there is no active transaction or
checkpoint. Under that constraint, the WAL refresh itself supplies
the required cross-process visibility probe; taking the database-file
shared lock and probing the rollback journal adds only overhead to
the hot read path.

The new helper:

1. Takes the inner lock once.
2. Defers to the general path if any active transaction or checkpoint
   is in flight (the fast path's invariants don't hold, so be safe).
3. Defers to the general path if `journal_mode != WAL` or the
   rollback-journal recovery state is pending (the fast path's
   invariants don't hold here either, so be safe).
4. Otherwise, runs only the WAL-side refresh
   (`refresh_committed_state`) plus a publication update keyed on
   whether `commit_seq` advanced. Skips the file-lock acquisition and
   the rollback-journal probe entirely.

`Connection::refresh_memdb_if_stale_with_publication_and_mode`
acquires the new helper via the new `clean_file_backed_memdb_
staleness_check` scenario id; the existing `memdb_staleness_check`
scenario remains on the general path. The dispatcher in
`PagerBackend::refresh_published_snapshot_for_clean_wal_read`
forwards to the matching pager flavor (Memory / IoUring / Unix /
Windows).

Hot-path profile counter (`FSQLITE_PAGER_PUBLICATION_REFRESHES`) is
still incremented on entry, so the existing observability for
publication refreshes still sees both fast and slow paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
